findings.title: Authentication Risk Detected
findings.description: High-risk keywords related to authentication have been detected in multiple sources, indicating potential weaknesses in the authentication flow that could lead to unauthorized access.
assumptions: Authentication mechanisms are critical for user data protection.

evidence.excerpt: Detected auth risk keywords in 4 source(s)

recommendations: Conduct a security review of the authentication flow to identify and remediate vulnerabilities.

findings.title: Potential PII Handling Issues
findings.description: The recent deployment may have inadvertently increased the handling of personally identifiable information (PII) without adequate consent mechanisms, violating data minimization principles.
assumptions: User consent is required for any PII collection.
recommendations: Implement a review of data collection practices and ensure all PII handling complies with consent requirements.

metadata.mitigation: Review and ensure compliance with consent mechanisms for PII.

findings.title: Accessibility Compliance Gaps
findings.description: Accessibility risk keywords detected indicate potential non-compliance with WCAG standards, which could hinder users with disabilities from effectively using the product.
assumptions: Accessibility is a requirement and must be prioritized.

evidence.excerpt: Detected accessibility risk keywords in 1 source(s)

recommendations: Conduct an accessibility audit to identify and remediate compliance gaps.

metadata.mitigation: Perform an accessibility audit and implement necessary changes.

summary: Identified 3 risks (1 accessibility, 1 privacy, 1 security). 2 flagged as blockers. Risk gate: FAILED (2 blockers, 1 warnings).
